name: Yorkshire Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1 clove garlic, cut in half lengthwise
1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons hot beef drippings (pan juices from the Rib Roast)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Beat the eggs until very light and fluffy.
3. Gradually beat in the flour and milk. Season with a pinch of salt, freshly ground black pepper, and 2 tablespoons of beef drippings.
4. Rub the pan (either a flat baking dish or muffin tins) with the cut side of the garlic.
5. Heat the pan in the oven.
6. Add the remaining 1/4 cup drippings to the pan and pour in the batter. If using muffin tins, fill each slot about 1/3 full with batter.
7. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes.
8. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until puddings are puffy and golden brown.
9. Do not open the oven during the first 20 minutes of baking. If you make one large pudding, cut into squares to serve.
